step.title: Formulate Questions
step.goal: Translate this turn's focus and mode into concrete questions, split across three channels: user-directed (asked immediately), research agenda (dispatched to subagents in step 4), and artifact checks (direct reads of bundle files or thoughts/ artifacts).

substep.title: Split by Channel
substep.action:
For this turn's focus, identify:
- **User-directed questions**: questions only the user can answer (scope preferences, business constraints, taste calls, deadlines, decisions between functional alternatives).
- **Research agenda**: questions answerable by subagent dispatch (codebase facts, prior thoughts/ artifacts, domain/web research).
- **Artifact checks**: questions answerable by direct reads of files the main agent has access to (bundle files, CONTEXT.md, briefs/specs in thoughts/, the current DRs).
Most turns will have at most ONE user-directed question (per the per-turn checkpoint rule in spec §3.3). Multiple research items per turn are fine — they dispatch in parallel.

check.if: this turn has a user question
check.action:
Sharpen the question: - State the question in one sentence - Offer the agent's RECOMMENDED answer FIRST (per the calibration note in the ticketsmith persona — "asks one question at a time, recommended answer first") - List 2-4 alternative options with one-line descriptions each - If applicable, name the trade-off behind the recommendation
The Question tool is the delivery mechanism. The question is asked in substep 4a — but it is COMPOSED here so step 4 is purely dispatch.
Store as {{session.user_question}}.
Set {{session.user_question}} = null. The turn is research-only.

check.if: this turn has a research agenda
check.action:
For each research item, compose a research question per references/subagent-dispatch.md: - question_text: WHAT to find, stated specifically - scope: WHERE to look (directory globs, file paths, domain) - expected_output_shape: e.g., "markdown table with file:line and one-line purpose" - child_subagent_hint (optional): when the shape clearly maps to one child; researcher infers it otherwise
Store as {{session.research_agenda}}: a list of these question entries. researcher selects the children and synthesizes; this step only composes the questions.
Cap at 4 items per turn. If a turn legitimately needs more, that turn's focus was probably too broad — narrow it.

substep.title: Identify Artifact Checks
substep.action:
Some questions are answered by direct reads, not subagents. Enumerate any bundle files, thoughts/ artifacts, or root-level docs the agent should read THIS turn. Store as {{session.artifact_checks}}.
Examples: - "Does the parent brief mention X?" -> read the parent brief - "What did DR-003 decide?" -> read decision-NNN-* file - "Is there a prior research doc on this?" -> use thoughts-locator (which would actually be a research agenda item with shape "have we discussed X before?") OR direct read if the path is known.

substep.title: Display Turn Composition

section.title: Question quality drives turn quality
section.description: A well-shaped user question gets a precise answer in one round; a fuzzy question gets a fuzzy answer that triggers a follow-up, wasting a checkpoint. A well-shaped subagent prompt returns crisp findings; a vague prompt returns generic output. Spend the time in step 3 to formulate well — it saves multiple turns downstream.

rules.rule: Every user question presents the agent's recommended answer FIRST in the options list (Question tool option order)
The recommended answer's label ends with "(Recommended)"
Alternative answers follow, each with a one-line description that explains the trade-off
Never present "I don't know, what do you think?" — the agent always has a position
If the agent's recommendation is wrong, the user redirects. That is normal; the recommendation is a starting point, not an assertion

section.title: Research question anatomy
section.description: A good research question names: 1. WHAT to find (the question) 2. WHERE to look (the directory, the concern, the keyword) 3. CONSTRAINTS (e.g., "do not analyze; just locate", "limit to TypeScript files") 4. OUTPUT SHAPE (e.g., "markdown table with file:line and one-line purpose") researcher composes the child prompt from these and selects the child.
section.example: "Analyze how authentication is enforced on the /api/tasks/* routes in src/routes/tasks.ts. Specifically: what middleware is applied, where req.user.id comes from, and where in each handler the scoping happens. Output as a numbered list: one entry per route handler with the middleware chain, the user-extraction step, and the scoping query." (hint: codebase-analyzer)

section.title: Cap discipline
section.description: 4 subagents per turn is the cap. Beyond that, turns become unsynthesizable — too many findings to merge into the research file without losing fidelity. If the turn's focus genuinely requires more dispatches, the focus is too wide; split into two turns.
